blog_title: Bali Trip from Kolkata: Upcoming Events Not to Miss in the Final Quarter of 2024
seo_title: Bali Trip from Kolkata: Upcoming Events Not to Miss in the Final Quarter of 2024
description: Discover the ultimate travel packing list for every adventure. Pack smart, light, and stress-free with essential tips .
body_content:
Published by travelxploria on Mon Jun 23 2025

In the final quarter of the year, Bali transforms into a vibrant hub of cultural festivities and unique events. It beckons travelers with many unique annual events during this period. Are you planning a trip from Kolkata to Bali during this time? You are in for an unforgettable experience.
Here are some top events that you can witness in Bali from October to December 2024.

Ubud Writers & Readers Festival (October)

This internationally acclaimed October event brings together writers, readers, thinkers, and artists from around the globe. It celebrates the power of words.

Set in the serene town of Ubud, the festival offers a series of panel discussions, workshops, and cultural performances. It is a perfect opportunity to engage with literary minds while soaking in the artistic ambiance of Bali.

Kuta Karnival (October)

Kuta Karnival lights up the lively beach town of Kuta with a burst of color and energy. This event is a celebration of Balinese culture, featuring traditional music and dance performances, food stalls, and vibrant parades.

It is a family-friendly festival where you can experience the local culture and enjoy the festive beach atmosphere.

Makepung Lampit Race (November)

November ushers in the unique Makepung Lampit Race, a traditional buffalo race held on flooded rice fields in Jembrana. This thrilling event showcases the strength and speed of the buffaloes, guided by skilled jockeys.

The race is a spectacular demonstration of Bali’s agrarian lifestyle and community spirit. Do not miss the lush rural landscapes of Bali as you enjoy this exciting competition.



Oronna Festival (November)

The Oronna Festival is a vibrant celebration of Balinese culture and heritage. Held in various villages across Bali, it features traditional dances, music, and rituals that reflect the island’s rich cultural diversity. You can witness local customs and enjoy the warm hospitality of the Balinese people.

Soma Ribek (November)

This traditional Balinese celebration dedicated to Dewi Sri, the goddess of rice and fertility. This November event involves rituals and offerings to express gratitude for the harvest. You can observe the spiritual practices and participate in the communal feasts that highlight the importance of agriculture in Balinese life.

Pagerwesi Ritual (November)

This significant spiritual event is focused on strengthening the soul and protecting oneself from evil forces. During this ritual, temples are adorned with offerings, and prayers are conducted to seek blessings. It is a profound cultural experience that will let you witness the deep spiritual roots of the Balinese community.

Denpasar Festival (December)

Held in the heart of Bali’s capital, the festival features art exhibitions, cultural performances, and culinary delights. It intertwines modern creativity with traditional customs, providing a perfect end to the year.

FAQ

How much can a Bali trip cost?

The cost will vary according to choice of accommodation, food, local transportation etc.

Is 50000 enough for Bali trip?

Yes, it can cover a budget-friendly Bali trip for a week.

How far is Bali from Kolkata by flight?

It is approximately 4,500 kilometers (2,796 miles).

From literary festivals and thrilling races to spiritual rituals and vibrant street parades, Bali offers something for every traveler in the final quarter of the year. Do not miss these exciting events!
